---
title: "Julia Engine"
subtitle: "High-performance numerical computing with native plots"
---

The **Julia engine** runs Julia via `IJulia`. Great when an algorithm needs raw
numerical speed. Plots use `Plots.jl` (GR backend). We set `GKSwstype=100` so GR
renders straight to a file on a headless machine.

## A worked example: IV bolus decay

```{julia}
#| label: fig-julia-pk
#| fig-cap: "Julia + Plots.jl: concentration–time curve."
using Plots
ENV["GKSwstype"] = "100"   # headless GR: write to file, no display server
gr()

dose, Vd, thalf = 1000.0, 50.0, 12.0
ke = log(2) / thalf
t  = 0:0.5:48
C  = (dose / Vd) .* exp.(-ke .* t)

plot(t, C, lw = 2.4, color = :firebrick, label = "C(t)",
     xlabel = "Time (h)", ylabel = "Concentration (mg/L)",
     title = "Julia — one-compartment IV bolus", legend = :topright)
hline!([5], ls = :dash, color = :grey, label = "MEC")
```

## An algorithm: Newton–Raphson root finding

Solve for the time at which concentration reaches the 5 mg/L threshold.
(The cell is self-contained.)

```{julia}
dose, Vd, ke = 1000.0, 50.0, log(2) / 12   # constants

f(x)  = (dose / Vd) * exp(-ke * x) - 5.0
df(x) = -ke * (dose / Vd) * exp(-ke * x)

x = 10.0
for _ in 1:20
    x -= f(x) / df(x)
end
println("Threshold crossed at t = ", round(x, digits = 2), " h")
println("Analytic solution      = ", round(log((dose / Vd) / 5) / ke, digits = 2), " h")
```
